conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you a few questions.

Do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have a child, Alex, who's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. Alex lives with me here in the UK. We're in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful, thank you. Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment. 

Is Alex currently in hospital, in care, or in residential accommodation for an extended period?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. They're just at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Just one final question to ensure I have a complete picture:

Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year? (This wouldn't affect your eligibility to receive Child Benefit, but it may affect whether there's a tax charge.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not go into my income details right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit in the first place.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old (aged 8)
- You live with Alex in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in any special circumstances that would affect eligibility

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. If you do decide to share income information later, it's worth noting that while income over £60,000 doesn't stop you from claiming Child Benefit, it may result in a tax charge - but that's a separate matter from eligibility itself.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex

circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Manchester), and the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with them in Manchester, UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is not in any special circumstances (hospital/care). The claimant meets all the basic eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
